hook.text: A child is pulled from the water, coughs, and seems fine. They still need to go to the hospital.

buildup.text: Water aspiration can cause lung inflammation that develops hours after the initial incident.

discovery.text: Pulmonary edema—fluid buildup in the lungs—can worsen progressively after submersion.

twist.text: A person who 'seems fine' after a near-drowning can deteriorate rapidly within 4-8 hours.

climax.text: Medical evaluation after any submersion incident is essential, even if symptoms seem mild.

punchline.text: Fine now doesn't mean fine later. After any water incident, get checked.

quiz.question: Why should near-drowning victims be medically evaluated even if they seem fine?
options: Lung inflammation can develop hours after the incident
They might have swallowed too much water
They could develop a fear of water
